title: Tropaeolum porifolium
taxonomy: Tropaeolum porifolium is a species of flowering plant, which is classified within the genus Tropaeolum and family Tropaeolaceae. This plant is native to the Andean region of South America. The Tropaeolum genus is part of the larger order Brassicales, which includes other flowering plants like cabbages and mustard. Within the Tropaeolum genus, Tropaeolum porifolium is one of many species, each with unique characteristics. The classification of Tropaeolum porifolium reflects its distinct evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.

intro: Tropaeolum porifolium, also known as the Nasturtium or Mountain Crest, is a flowering plant native to South America, belonging to the Tropaeolaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tropaeolum porifolium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, tinctures, and creams, often used to soothe skin irritations and relieve joint pain.
